---
title: "Climate vulnerability as a driver of forced-labour risk"
description: "Invited talk presenting cross-sectional evidence linking climate exposure to modern slavery prevalence in food-system supply chains."
date: 2026-02-18
categories: [invited-talk, climate, modern-slavery]
venue: "International Conference on Sustainability Science"
location: "Berlin, Germany"
slides: "#"
---

[← Back to Conferences](../conferences.html){.back-link}

<div class="label" style="margin-bottom: 16px;">Invited Talk · ICSS 2026 · Berlin</div>

## Abstract

I presented evidence from a cross-sectional analysis of 60 producing economies showing that climate-vulnerability indices are strongly correlated with downstream forced-labour risk in food-system supply chains, even after controlling for governance quality and HDI. The talk argued for treating climate adaptation and labour-rights protection as a single design problem.

## Details

| | |
|---|---|
| **Venue** | International Conference on Sustainability Science (ICSS 2026) |
| **Location** | Berlin, Germany |
| **Date** | 18 February 2026 |
| **Format** | 30-min invited talk + 15-min Q&A |

## Materials

- [Slides (PDF)](#)
- [Recorded talk](#) (if available)
